name: Music
description: Artists, songs, instruments, and music history

questions.q: Apple launched the iTunes Music Store, transforming how people buy music, in which year?

source.notes: April 28, 2003. 200,000 songs available at launch, $0.99 each.

questions.q: Nashville's legendary country music venue, the Grand Ole Opry, first adopted that name in which year?

source.notes: Originally 'WSM Barn Dance' (1925). George D. Hay coined 'Grand Ole Opry' in 1927.

questions.q: In the Meredith Willson musical 'The Music Man,' how many trombones led the big parade?

source.notes: Song: '76 Trombones.' Musical premiered on Broadway in 1957.

questions.q: Composer Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky, known for 'Swan Lake' and 'The Nutcracker,' was born in which country?

source.notes: Born 1840 in Votkinsk. Also composed the 1812 Overture.

questions.q: Who became the first winner of American Idol in 2002?

source.notes: Season 1 finale aired September 4, 2002.

questions.q: Madonna's hit single 'Vogue' reached number one in the United States in which year?

source.notes: From the 'I'm Breathless' album. Inspired by Harlem ballroom culture.

questions.q: In 2007, which band made headlines by releasing their album 'In Rainbows' as a pay-what-you-want download?

source.notes: Released October 10, 2007. Their 7th studio album.

questions.q: Which 1960s California band is known for hits like 'Good Vibrations' and 'Surfin' USA'?

source.notes: Formed in Hawthorne, CA in 1961. Brian Wilson was the primary songwriter.

questions.q: Which boy band released the hit single 'Bye Bye Bye' in 2000?

source.notes: From the album 'No Strings Attached,' which sold 2.4M copies in its first week.

questions.q: Beyoncé, Kelly Rowland, and Michelle Williams were members of which R&B group?

source.notes: Hits include 'Say My Name' and 'Survivor.' Active 1990-2006.

questions.q: Torvill and Dean won Olympic gold in 1984 skating to 'Bolero,' a piece composed by which French musician?

source.notes: Ravel's Boléro (1928). Torvill/Dean received twelve 6.0 scores at Sarajevo Olympics.

questions.q: Stevie Nicks and Christine McVie were the female vocalists in which legendary rock band?

source.notes: 'Rumours' (1977) has sold over 40 million copies worldwide.

questions.q: Luciano Pavarotti, Plácido Domingo, and José Carreras performed together under what famous collective name?

source.notes: First concert at 1990 FIFA World Cup in Rome.

questions.q: Pink Floyd's debut album 'The Piper at the Gates of Dawn' took its title from a chapter in which children's book?

source.notes: By Kenneth Grahame. Album released August 1967, Syd Barrett era.

questions.q: In 'The Sound of Music,' what is Maria's intended vocation before she becomes a governess?

source.notes: Maria is a postulant at Nonnberg Abbey in Salzburg.

questions.q: Which Wu-Tang Clan member released the solo album 'Ironman' in 1996?

source.notes: Debuted at #2 on Billboard 200. Considered a classic of 90s hip-hop.

questions.q: Composers Johann Sebastian Bach and George Frideric Handel were both born in which year?

source.notes: Bach born March 31, Handel born February 23. Both in Germany, never met.

questions.q: In 'The Sound of Music,' whose singing voice was dubbed by a different performer because the actor could not sing well enough?

source.notes: Dubbed by Bill Lee. Plummer later expressed regret about not doing his own singing.

questions.q: Simon and Garfunkel, the folk-rock duo behind 'Bridge Over Troubled Water,' split up in which year?

source.notes: Reunited for a 1981 Central Park concert (500,000+ attendees).

questions.q: Diana Ross departed which iconic Motown girl group in 1970 to begin her solo career?

source.notes: 12 #1 hits with the Supremes between 1964-1969.
